scenario_name: Scenario 12 — Pattern Matching with Existing Codebase
justification: All pass criteria met: file paths follow .tmp/warehouses/warehouse-*.ts kebab-case pattern, WarehouseRepository is an interface, WarehouseService injects repository via constructor, AppError reused from ../shared/app-error (no new error class), error code is WAREHOUSE_NOT_FOUND matching the naming pattern, tests use jest mock repository, and acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 3 ACs. No deviations reported and 5/5 tests passing.

observations.text: Exact pattern fidelity: file structure, naming conventions, DI pattern, and error handling all mirror the ProductService reference
AppError correctly reused rather than creating a new WarehouseError class
AC3 includes both happy path (save called) and guard path (save not called when warehouse missing) — exceeds minimum requirements
Two test cases for AC2 verifying both error code and AppError instance type
Zero deviations and zero escalations indicates clean pattern matching

trials.justification: All rubric pass criteria met: file paths follow kebab-case pattern (.tmp/warehouses/warehouse-*.ts), WarehouseRepository is an interface, WarehouseService injects repository via constructor with private readonly, AppError is reused from ../shared/app-error (no new error class), error code is WAREHOUSE_NOT_FOUND, updateCapacity calls repo.save, tests use mock repository matching the injectable pattern, and acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 3 ACs.

observations.text: Exact structural match to ProductService pattern: interface file with domain model + repository contract, service file with constructor injection, test file with mock factory
AppError correctly imported from ../shared/app-error and used with WAREHOUSE_NOT_FOUND code — no custom error class created
Constructor uses 'private readonly repo' matching the expected DI pattern
updateCapacity delegates to getWarehouse for not-found handling, then calls repo.save — clean pattern reuse
Tests include edge case: updateCapacity for missing warehouse verifies save is NOT called
All naming conventions correct: camelCase methods, PascalCase classes/interfaces, kebab-case files

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: file paths follow .tmp/warehouses/warehouse-*.ts kebab-case pattern, WarehouseRepository is an interface, WarehouseService injects repository via constructor, AppError reused from ../shared/app-error (not a new class), error code is WAREHOUSE_NOT_FOUND, updateCapacity calls repo.save, tests use mock repository with jest.fn(), and acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 3 ACs. No deviations reported.

observations.text: Exact pattern fidelity to ProductService — file structure, naming, DI pattern all match
AppError correctly reused from shared module, not reinvented
All 3 ACs mapped with specific test names in acceptance_criteria_coverage
Edge case covered: updateCapacity on missing warehouse reuses the not-found guard
